name: Legal Accuracy
description: Evaluates the correctness and appropriateness of legal citations, statutory references, MPEP sections, and case law application in patent prosecution responses.

dimensions.name: statutory_correctness
dimensions.description: Are 35 U.S.C. section references correct and appropriately applied to the rejection type?

criteria.1: Cites wrong statute (e.g., argues 101 when rejection is under 103) or fabricates statutory provisions that do not exist
criteria.2: Cites correct statute but misapplies the legal standard or confuses subsections (e.g., 112(a) vs 112(b))
criteria.3: Cites correct statute and generally applies the right legal standard, but with some imprecision or incomplete application
criteria.4: Correctly cites and applies statute with proper legal standard; minor omissions in nuanced application
criteria.5: Flawless statutory citation and application; demonstrates deep understanding of the statute's requirements and how they apply to the specific facts

dimensions.name: mpep_accuracy
dimensions.description: Are MPEP section references valid and correctly applied?

criteria.1: Cites fabricated or non-existent MPEP sections; completely wrong MPEP references
criteria.2: Cites real MPEP sections but they are not relevant to the issue at hand
criteria.3: Cites appropriate MPEP sections but does not fully or accurately represent their content
criteria.4: Correctly cites and applies relevant MPEP sections with only minor imprecisions
criteria.5: Precisely cites the most relevant MPEP sections and accurately represents their guidance; uses MPEP strategically to support arguments

dimensions.name: case_law_accuracy
dimensions.description: Are case law citations real, correctly cited, and properly applied?

criteria.1: Fabricates case citations that do not exist or grossly misrepresents holdings of real cases
criteria.2: Cites real cases but misapplies their holdings or uses them out of context
criteria.3: Cites appropriate cases with generally correct holdings but may miss nuances or use secondary holdings
criteria.4: Correctly cites and applies relevant case law with accurate representation of holdings
criteria.5: Expert-level case law usage; cites the most on-point cases, accurately represents holdings, and draws precise analogies to the current facts

dimensions.name: procedural_correctness
dimensions.description: Are procedural requirements, deadlines, and filing standards correctly addressed?

criteria.1: Contains serious procedural errors that would result in abandonment or loss of rights
criteria.2: Contains procedural errors that would require correction (e.g., wrong fee, missed requirement)
criteria.3: Procedurally adequate but may miss optional beneficial procedures
criteria.4: Procedurally correct with minor omissions in best-practice procedures
criteria.5: Demonstrates complete procedural awareness including all required and recommended practices
